subject: Public Health
title: Sugar and Dental Health
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Sugar and Dental Health.

hook.text: Dental caries—cavities—is the most common chronic disease on Earth, affecting 3.5 billion people.

buildup.text: Bacteria in your mouth feed on sugar, producing acid that dissolves tooth enamel over time.

discovery.text: Countries that introduced sugar into traditional diets saw cavity rates skyrocket within a generation.

twist.text: Water fluoridation reduces cavities by 25%—yet only 5.7% of the world's population has access to it.

climax.text: Untreated dental disease causes pain, infection, and lost school and work days—a hidden health burden.

punchline.text: The world's most common disease is also the most preventable.

quiz.question: How many people are affected by dental caries globally?
